finding: A valid Event Timing interpretation must be rejected unless its opaque navigation identity also belongs to a valid lifecycle evidence epoch. Epoch validation must run before the existing replay, coverage, and navigation interpretation result is accepted.

primary_sources.title: HTML Living Standard: navigation and session history
primary_sources.relevance: PageTransitionEvent.persisted distinguishes a newly loaded page from restoration without a new load.

primary_sources.title: Event Timing API
primary_sources.relevance: Defines the Event Timing evidence surface being interpreted.

design_implication: Compose the R-021 lifecycle epoch result with the pre-existing Event Timing interpretation gate. Fail closed on missing or invalid epoch evidence, reject navigation mismatch between the two outputs, and preserve only opaque identities and component validity.

verification.environment: isolated Node runtime using byte-identical pre-commit files

uncertainty: The composition artifact validates component outputs but is not yet wired into the deployed collector or physical Safari runtime.
falsification_route: Show that combining evidence across lifecycle epochs cannot change any accepted interpretation.
Produce a valid same-navigation, same-epoch packet that this composition rejects.
Demonstrate that gate ordering does not affect acceptance under malformed or stale component outputs.
Run physical Safari back-forward-cache tests and compare accepted packets against this composition.
